note: Shared persona metadata for schedule-personas.html and personas.html. Edit descriptions here — they're loaded at runtime.

Andrey.description: Older adults, dementia, domestic and cultural context of aging. Mixed methods, field deployments. Interested in how technology supports agency and everyday life across later years.

Ava.description: Design fiction, futures, and critical provocations. Cares about the questions a design raises. Works across pictorials, zines, and discursive artefacts.

Chris.description: Journalism, policy, civic engagement, and accountability. Works with NGOs, community groups, and government partners on stakeholder-facing design.

Emily.description: Designs with microbial, plant, animal, and AI kin. Posthumanist leanings — design as coexistence work. Draws from speculative design, craft, and environmental humanities.

Greg.description: Builds LLM-powered tools and studies their use in creative and knowledge work. Comfortable with prompt engineering, agent architectures, and generative pipelines.

Haley.description: Clinical contexts, chronic illness, caregiving, and mental health. Works with clinicians, patients, and their networks; values ecological validity and long-term deployments.

Jen.description: Community-led, participatory, equity-oriented. Cares about reflexivity, power, and whose voices shape the design.

Karthik.description: Social robots, expressive robotics, and human-robot collaboration. Designs for industrial, domestic, and performance contexts.

Martin.description: Motor learning, haptics, and skill training in music, dance, and sport. Multi-modal feedback systems and AR/VR for skill acquisition.

Matt.description: Textiles, ceramics, 3D print, knitting, and e-textiles. Studio-based practice; values tacit knowledge and material agency.

Paula.description: Visual-first research communication. Craft-as-inquiry, research through image. Engages deeply with the pictorial track.

Rich.description: Builds novel interactive systems and interaction techniques. Evaluates them through controlled user studies, with attention to study design, counterbalancing, and dependent measures.

Sydney.description: Works from felt experience and somaesthetics. Values bodily knowledge — autoethnography, micro-phenomenology, sensibilities as data.

Terry.description: Works with blind / low-vision, deaf, dyslexic, and neurodivergent communities. Centers co-design grounded in lived experience.

Warren.description: VR / AR / MR narrative, presence, and embodiment. Comfortable with immersive production and game-engine pipelines.
